complaint_text: I noticed three transactions on my Visa credit card totalling $4,280 that I did not authorise. The charges were made at electronics stores in Sydney while I was overseas.
My home was damaged in the January floods. I lodged a claim for $45,000 in repairs but the insurer denied it saying the damage was caused by 'rising water' not 'storm damage'.
I lost my job and requested a 3-month repayment pause on my home loan. The bank refused and offered to extend my loan by 5 years costing $85,000 extra in interest.
Someone opened a credit card in my name using stolen identity documents and accumulated $15,000 in charges. The bank approved the application without adequate identity verification.
I was approved for a $750,000 home loan despite having an income of only $85,000 and existing debts of $60,000. The bank failed its responsible lending obligations.
My insurer offered $22,000 for my written-off vehicle but comparable listings show it is worth $28,000. They used automated valuation ignoring low mileage and condition.
My bank sent my complete financial statements to another customer with the same surname. This is a serious privacy breach and I have suffered distress.
My income protection claim was denied because the insurer says my condition does not meet the policy definition of total disability. My psychiatrist disagrees.
I was charged a $450 annual fee on my platinum credit card despite calling to downgrade my card two months prior. The bank has no record of my call.
I was scammed into transferring $18,500 to a fraudulent investment platform. I reported the scam within 2 hours but my bank says they cannot recover the funds.
My superannuation death benefit is being distributed to my partner's ex-wife instead of me. We have been in a de facto relationship for 8 years and I am the listed beneficiary.
My credit card payments are being allocated to the lowest interest rate balance instead of the highest, contrary to ASIC credit card reforms from 2019.
A debt collector has been calling me 3-4 times daily and contacted my employer. The debt is disputed and their conduct breaches ACCC guidelines.
My home insurance premium increased by 42% at renewal with no explanation. I have never made a claim in 12 years.
My mortgage broker recommended a loan product with a honeymoon rate that reverted to significantly above market. The broker received a higher commission on this product.
My financial adviser switched my super from a low-cost industry fund to a retail fund with fees going from $680 to $2,400 per year without explaining the difference.
My bank's mobile app was down for 3 days. This resulted in dishonoured direct debits costing $60 in fees and a late payment on my credit report.
My life insurance claim was denied under the suicide exclusion clause but the coroner made no finding of intentional self-harm. My husband died in a vehicle accident.
My insurer is deducting 30% from my claim for roof replacement due to betterment but my policy is new-for-old and there is no betterment clause in the PDS.
My ex-partner took out an additional $50,000 on our joint home loan without my knowledge. The bank processed a forged signature.
I cancelled my car insurance mid-term and was charged a $300 cancellation fee plus a short-rate penalty reducing my refund by 40%. The PDS doesn't disclose the calculation.
My Total and Permanent Disability claim through my super fund has been in assessment for 14 months. I have provided all medical evidence but the fund keeps requesting more reports.
My business interruption insurance claim for COVID-19 was denied despite my policy not containing a pandemic exclusion, contrary to the AFCA test case ruling.
I applied for early release of my superannuation on compassionate grounds for urgent medical treatment but the fund has delayed 8 weeks requesting documents I already provided.
My bank restricted access to my $45,000 home loan redraw facility without notice. I need the funds for medical expenses urgently.
My travel insurance claim for $8,500 medical expenses was denied for non-disclosure of asthma, but I declared it on the form and paid an additional premium.
My bank continued direct debiting my account after I cancelled the arrangement. Three unauthorised debits of $580 each caused my account to go into overdraft.
My credit card loyalty program earn rate was halved from 1.5 to 0.75 points per dollar with only 30 days notice. I pay $395 annual fee specifically for this earn rate.
My business cyber insurance claim for ransomware was denied because I allegedly didn't maintain reasonable security measures. I had antivirus and regular backups.
I purchased furniture for $3,500 that was never delivered. The merchant went into liquidation. My bank refuses to process a chargeback despite being within Visa timeframes.
